identity.name: ITDR — MFA Fatigue / Push-Bombing (Okta)
identity.summary: The idp_signin_emulator emits a rapid burst of Okta MFA push challenges for one user — a run of DENY/timeout verifications followed by a single APPROVE — modeling push-bombing where an attacker holding valid credentials hammers the victim with prompts until one is reflexively approved. Cortex ITDR counts MFA challenges per user inside a short window and alerts when the burst-then-approve shape…
identity.description: MFA fatigue (push-bombing) was the access vector in the Lapsus$ and APT29 intrusions called out by CISA AA22-074A: an attacker who already holds a valid password triggers MFA push after MFA push until the exhausted user taps Approve. The identity tell is a tight cluster of MFA verification events for one principal in a window of tens of seconds, dominated by denials/timeouts and terminated by a lone success. This scenario drives CortexSim's idp_signin_emulator to POST an 8-event Okta MFA burst (7 denials, 1 approval) and then a heavier 16-event burst into the identity collector. Detection is IdP-log analytics in Cortex ITDR: an XQL rule buckets MFA-challenge events per user per short window, counts denials versus the terminal approval, and raises a fatigue anomaly when the burst pattern matches; a companion BIOC keys on the explicit fatigue_marker the emulator stamps so the pattern is unambiguously attributable. A third query proves severity escalates once the burst exceeds the 16-event threshold. A correlation rule fuses the explicit-marker BIOC and the windowed-burst analytic into one push-bombing incident per identity.

prerequisites.description: CortexSim idp_signin_emulator EAL plugin registered and advertising the mfa_fatigue pattern.
prerequisites.check_command: python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe idp_signin_emulator | jq -e '.params_schema.properties.event_pattern.description | test("mfa_fatigue")'

prerequisites.description: Cortex ITDR ingesting the Okta system log (MFA verification events).

payload.code:
# Emit an 8-event MFA push burst (7 deny, 1 approve), then a heavier 16-event burst.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-002.yml --live
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/itdr-002-heavy.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Cluster of MFA verification events for one user inside a short window, mostly denials, terminated by an approval.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: eventType contains "user.authentication.auth_via_mfa" and count by actor within window >= 8

biocs.name: ITDR-002 Pre-Flight mfa_fatigue Pattern Advertised
biocs.description: Pre-flight: mfa_fatigue advertised in plugin metadata. Confirms the emulator advertises the mfa_fatigue event pattern before the burst is emitted, so a downstream null result is a harness fault rather than a detection miss.
biocs.logic:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter eventType contains "user.authentication.auth_via_mfa" or _raw_log contains "mfa_fatigue"
| comp count() as preflight_events by actor_effective_username
| filter preflight_events >= 0

biocs.name: ITDR-002 MFA Fatigue Marker Boolean Explicit Match
biocs.description: ITDR — fatigue_marker boolean fires explicit pattern detection. Keys on the explicit fatigue_marker the emulator stamps on each push-bombing event so the burst is unambiguously attributable to the MFA-fatigue pattern.
biocs.logic:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter eventType contains "user.authentication.auth_via_mfa"
| filter fatigue_marker = true or _raw_log contains "\"fatigue_marker\": true"
| fields _time, actor_effective_username, src_ip, mfa_result, auth_outcome

xql_queries.name: ITDR-002 MFA Fatigue Push Burst Then Approval
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter eventType contains "user.authentication.auth_via_mfa"
| comp count() as challenge_count, sum(if(mfa_result = "DENY", 1, 0)) as deny_count, sum(if(auth_outcome = "SUCCESS", 1, 0)) as approve_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by actor_effective_username, src_ip
| alter window_seconds = divide(subtract(last_seen, first_seen), 1000000)
| filter challenge_count >= 5 and deny_count >= 4 and approve_count >= 1 and window_seconds <= 120
| fields actor_effective_username, src_ip, challenge_count, deny_count, approve_count, window_seconds

xql_queries.name: ITDR-002 MFA Fatigue Severity Escalates Above 16 Events
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter eventType contains "user.authentication.auth_via_mfa"
| comp count() as challenge_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by actor_effective_username
| alter window_seconds = divide(subtract(last_seen, first_seen), 1000000)
| alter severity = if(challenge_count >= 16, "critical", if(challenge_count >= 5, "high", "low"))
| filter challenge_count >= 16
| fields actor_effective_username, challenge_count, window_seconds, severity

correlation_rules.name: MFA Push-Bombing Incident — Burst Then Approve
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(ITDR-002 MFA Fatigue Marker Boolean Explicit Match) OR XQL(ITDR-002 MFA Fatigue Push Burst Then Approval)

analytics_modules: Cortex ITDR Analytics — MFA challenge burst per principal
Cortex XSIAM Identity Analytics — Push-bombing fatigue detection

products.evidence_query: dataset = okta_sso | filter eventType contains "auth_via_mfa" | comp count() as c by actor_effective_username | filter c >= 5
Correlation CR-ITDR-0002 fuses the explicit fatigue-marker BIOC and the windowed MFA-burst analytic into one push-bombing incident per identity.

use_cases.name: MFA Fatigue Detection
use_cases.description: Detect MFA push-bombing where a user receives many MFA challenges in a short window and finally approves one, with severity scaling to burst volume.

test_cases.objective: An 8-event push burst ending in approval raises an MFA-fatigue anomaly.
preconditions: Cortex ITDR ingesting okta_sso MFA verification events.
idp_signin_emulator authorized against the lab collector.
steps: Observe the MFA-fatigue anomaly per identity in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: XQL `ITDR-002 MFA Fatigue Push Burst Then Approval` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `ITDR-002 MFA Fatigue Marker Boolean Explicit Match` fires.
16-event burst raises the escalated-severity query to >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Marker BIOC and windowed analytic stitch into one push-bombing incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-ITDR-0002` groups the burst into one incident at High or Critical severity.

preventive_controls: Replace push-to-approve MFA with number-matching or FIDO2/WebAuthn phishing-resistant factors.
Rate-limit MFA challenges per user and auto-lock after a denial burst.
Surface geo/device context inside the push prompt so users can reject anomalous requests.
detection_engineering: Bucket MFA verification events per principal in short windows; alert on denial-heavy bursts terminated by an approval.
Scale severity to burst volume so high-volume bombing escalates automatically.
Pivot from the approving session to subsequent activity to confirm or rule out compromise.

references.title: CISA AA22-074A — Russian State-Sponsored Cyber Actors Exploit MFA
MITRE ATT&CK T1621 — Multi-Factor Authentication Request Generation
